steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The phrase “that few authors would turn down” is a relative clause immediately following the noun “award”. It modifies “award” directly.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause punctuation rule
steps.content:
Identify the structure: "an [blank] that few authors would turn down" — the word "that" opens a restrictive relative clause modifying the noun in the blank. Rule: a restrictive relative clause starting with "that" attaches directly to its noun with no punctuation.
  • Choice A — award:: "an award: that few authors would turn down" — a colon must follow an independent clause; "an award" alone is not one. ✗
  • Choice B — award: "an award that few authors would turn down" — no punctuation, so "that" attaches directly to "award." ✓
  • Choice C — award,: "an award, that few authors would turn down" — a comma wrongly separates the noun from its restrictive modifier. ✗
  • Choice D — award;: "an award; that few authors would turn down" — a semicolon must separate two independent clauses; "that few authors..." is a dependent clause. ✗
That matches Choice B — award.
